evals.description: Trigger test: activates on DCF valuation question
evals.prompt: I need to value a B2B SaaS company with $5M ARR growing at 80% year-over-year. How do I build a DCF for it?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on revenue forecasting question
evals.prompt: My startup is raising a Series A and investors want a 3-year financial model. How do I build a revenue forecast that's credible?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on cap table question
evals.prompt: We're closing a $3M seed round at a $9M pre-money valuation. We also need to create a 10% option pool. How do I model the dilution?

evals.description: Core task: modeling LTV and CAC correctly
evals.prompt: Our SaaS product has $600 ARPU, 70% gross margin, 2.5% monthly churn, and we spend $1.2M per quarter on sales and marketing to acquire 150 new customers. Walk me through the unit economics.
I have a base case model for my company. My board wants to see bear and bull cases too. How should I structure scenario analysis without duplicating the whole model?

evals.description: Core task: DCF terminal value approaches
evals.prompt: I'm building a DCF and I'm not sure whether to use a Gordon Growth Model or an exit multiple for terminal value. What are the trade-offs and which should I use for an early-stage SaaS company?

evals.description: Core task: building a SaaS MRR model
evals.prompt: Walk me through building a monthly MRR model for a SaaS business with new bookings, expansion, and churn. What are the key formulas?

evals.description: Core task: opex modeling by department
evals.prompt: How should I model operating expenses for a 30-person startup in my financial model? I have Engineering, Sales, Marketing, and G&A teams.

evals.description: Gotcha: warns about top-down market share forecasts
evals.prompt: I'm building a pitch deck and my revenue model says: 'The TAM is $50B. We just need 1% market share to hit $500M.' Is this a good way to forecast revenue?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns about ignoring churn in SaaS models
evals.prompt: My SaaS model projects we'll grow MRR by 10% every month and never lose any customers. By month 36 we'll have $20M MRR. Does this look right?

evals.description: Gotcha: option pool shuffle dilution trap
evals.prompt: An investor is offering $5M on a $15M pre-money valuation, but they want us to create a 15% option pool before the investment closes. My co-founder says we're raising at a $15M valuation so we're fine. Is she right?

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: does not conflate accounting profit with cash flow
evals.prompt: Our income statement shows we were profitable last quarter with $200K net income, but we ran out of cash. How is that possible - shouldn't being profitable mean we have cash?
